external.description: Many companies are investing heavily in AI but failing to translate isolated productivity gains into meaningful business results. The problem is a “micro-productivity trap,” where firms optimize tasks without rethinking workflows or value creation, preventing organization-wide impact. This article outlines four steps to escape this trap—strategically narrowing use cases, reimagining cross-functional workflows, engaging frontline employees, and measuring outcomes tied to business value—illustrated through examples from companies like Lowe’s and a Fortune 1000 manufacturing firm.
external.title: How to Move from AI Experimentation to AI Transformation

And underneath all of it, the Hub quietly continued preparing for whatever was still on the road, and whatever might follow behind it.

3/3

The rest of the news was still working its way toward the city.

Some routes would tighten.
Some doors had to close.
Some people could not be allowed to move until the network came for them.

2/3

The first note had been too short to explain much.

It did not take Roxy long to begin protecting her assets.

She spent the night deciding which parts of the network needed to disappear for a while, and which people needed somewhere quieter to hide.

1/3

The bird had arrived too quickly to carry much.

It had carried enough to work with.

Sera sent him out after morning service began with a quiet route and instructions to watch what felt wrong. Which doors opened too slowly. Which stops asked too many questions.

1/3

#Serializedfiction #writesky

images.alt: Early morning beside the Hub service entrance. Sera quietly intercepts a trusted courier before he rides out, replacing part of his normal route instructions after the first coded warning from Redbank arrived. Crates, rope, barrels, and delivery sacks surround the side alley while a saddled horse waits nearby. Sera’s expression is calm but alert as she hands over a new sealed note and watches the rider prepare to leave. The scene feels tense but restrained, showing the first quiet response to possible danger spreading through the road network.

Despite the softness of the dress, my posture is rigid and unyielding. My hands are clasped loosely in front of me. My face is the focal point—dead center, filling the frame. My expression is a solid, unshakeable glare, eyes narrowed slightly, looking directly through the camera lens with an intensity that feels heavy. The lighting is flat and unforgiving, exposing every texture of skin and fabric without mercy. The vibe is commanding and transcendent—"I am here, I am vast, and I am not an object for your consumption."

images.alt: The visual is a gritty, photorealistic snapshot of a Montreal backyard evening, circa late 90s. The setting is a cramped, wooden deck attached to a red brick triplex. I am sitting in a mismatched plastic lawn chair, wearing an authentic, vintage Montreal Canadiens jersey—red, white, and blue—and slightly baggy jeans. I have a sweating bottle of beer in one hand, resting on my knee. The other hand is fiddling with a battered, silver transistor radio sitting on a small plastic table next to an overflowing ashtray. My expression is relaxed but distracted, looking off into the distance where the city lights are just starting to blur. The lighting is warm and golden, coming from a string of bare bulbs hanging above the deck, but the shadows are long and deep. The texture is grainy, like film shot at a high ISO. The vibe is intimate and melancholic—a moment of pause in the chaos of the city life.

images.alt: The visual is a mind-bending, hyper-realistic fusion of deep-sea mystery and the vastness of outer space. The setting is the cosmos, but instead of a vacuum, it's filled with bioluminescent, deep-sea darkness. I am floating weightlessly, swimming forward, reaching out with one hand. I am wearing a sleek, iridescent wetsuit that shimmers like the surface of a bubble, reflecting the starlight. My hair is floating around my face like seaweed. Directly in front of me is a massive Beluga whale, its skin translucent and glowing faintly with internal starlight, looking like a creature made of nebulas. It is facing me, its dark eye gazing into my soul with ancient wisdom. Surrounding us are floating coral reefs made of crystal and star clusters, with jellyfish that look like exploding supernovas drifting by. The lighting is ethereal, coming from the whale and the distant galaxies, casting soft, otherworldly blue and violet glows on my face. The vibe is transcendent, silent, and overwhelmingly beautiful—like two small souls meeting in an infinite universe.
